name: TheOrchardCoreTheme.Content
description: Seeds the home LandingPage (Hero, LogoStrip, Statistics, Cards Grid, Feature Explorer, Comparison, Showcase, Get Started, FAQs, Event Promo blocks), deploys the media, seeds the SiteStatistics singleton, and sets the home page.
author: Orchard Core

Layers.Description: Widgets shown on every page, for all cultures.

Layers.Name: English

ContentItems.DisplayText: The open-source .NET CMS and application framework.

Lead.Html: Orchard Core is a complete CMS for your editors and an application framework for your developers. Content, users and roles, media, search, and workflows are built in. Free, open-source and community led.

CodeSnippet.Text:
[comment]# Create a new Orchard Core site and run it[/comment]
$ [cmd]dotnet[/cmd] new install [name]OrchardCore.ProjectTemplates[/name]
$ [cmd]dotnet[/cmd] new occms [flag]-n[/flag] [name]MySite[/name]
$ [cmd]cd[/cmd] [name]MySite[/name] && [cmd]dotnet[/cmd] run

TitlePart.Title: The open-source .NET CMS and application framework.

Lead.Html: Orchard Core is both a complete CMS and an application framework. It is open-source and community-led, with more than fifteen years behind it. Here are a few of the reasons teams choose it.

Body.Html: Built for speed from the start. Sites are quick out of the box, with no extra caching modules to bolt on.
Built on ASP.NET Core and the Microsoft stack, with two-factor authentication and fine-grained roles and permissions built in.
Free and BSD-3-Clause licensed, with no hidden costs. No license fees, no per-seat or per-site pricing, and no upsell for features that should be free.
Backed by the .NET Foundation, with a 15-year lineage. No single company owns it, and it has never been relicensed.
Built on mainstream .NET, so you hire from a large pool of developers, not a niche skill set.
Editors model content, manage media, and publish in many languages, all without code.
Built for speed from the start. Sites are quick out of the box, with no extra caching modules to bolt on.
Built on ASP.NET Core and the Microsoft stack, with two-factor authentication and fine-grained roles and permissions built in.
Build with C# and Razor, and host it anywhere .NET runs.
Orchard Core is modular by design, so you add features with your own modules.
BSD-3-Clause licensed, with the full source in your hands. No license fees and no lock-in.
From a content site to a headless API or a large multi-tenant app, with no re-platforming.

Text.Html: Meet the community that builds Orchard Core, or browse the sites already running on it.

ContentItems.DisplayText: The features you need, built in.

Title.Text: The features you need, built in.

Lead.Html: Over 90 modules ship with the project and are maintained alongside the core. Here are a few of the highlights. Enable them as you need them.

Description.Html: Model your content without code. Build and change content types, parts, and fields from the admin, with no migrations to write.

MediaTexts: Content Types in the Orchard Core admin

Description.Html: Sign users in with external providers and single sign-on, and turn on two-factor authentication.

MediaTexts: OpenID Connect settings in the Orchard Core admin

Description.Html: Upload, process, and serve images and files. Resize on the fly, store on remote providers, and lock down folders with permissions.

MediaTexts: Media Management in the Orchard Core admin

Description.Html: Automate your site with a visual workflow editor. Trigger flows from content events, HTTP requests, or timers.

MediaTexts: The Workflows visual editor in the Orchard Core admin

Description.Html: Control who can do what. Assign roles to users and grant fine-grained permissions, with each feature adding its own.

MediaTexts: Roles & Permissions in the Orchard Core admin

Description.Html: Run your site in many languages. Localize both content and the admin UI, with culture pickers and per-user cultures.

MediaTexts: Localization settings in the Orchard Core admin

Description.Html: Query your content over a typed GraphQL API. Build headless sites and apps with no extra code.

MediaTexts: The GraphQL API explorer in the Orchard Core admin

Description.Html: Run many isolated sites from one install. Each tenant has its own content, users, and settings, on a single codebase.

MediaTexts: Multi-tenancy in the Orchard Core admin

Text.Html: Orchard Core ships with over 90 modules, and you can build your own. See how it compares with Umbraco and ABP.

Lead.Html: If you have already chosen .NET, these are the two tools you are most likely to weigh up. Umbraco is a CMS, ABP is an application framework, and Orchard Core is both.

ContentItems.DisplayText: A .NET application framework. CMS via a module: basic free, Pro paid

TitlePart.Title: A .NET application framework. CMS via a module: basic free, Pro paid

ContentItems.DisplayText: Content sites and applications, on one platform

TitlePart.Title: Content sites and applications, on one platform

ContentItems.DisplayText: Open-source, with no paid tier and no extra module licenses or fees

TitlePart.Title: Open-source, with no paid tier and no extra module licenses or fees

ContentItems.DisplayText: Open-source core, with a paid cloud and add-ons

TitlePart.Title: Open-source core, with a paid cloud and add-ons

ContentItems.DisplayText: Open-source core, with paid commercial tiers

TitlePart.Title: Open-source core, with paid commercial tiers

Text.Html: Both are strong tools. Umbraco has a larger editor ecosystem and a polished commercial cloud, and ABP goes deeper on architecture for big business applications. Orchard Core's place is doing both content and applications on one open, community-run platform.

ContentItems.DisplayText: A project with people behind it.

Title.Text: A project with people behind it.

Lead.Html: Hundreds of developers build Orchard Core together, and the community is friendly and quick to help. Here is where to ask questions, learn, and get involved.

Body.Html: A great place to ask the community and get help from the people who build and use Orchard Core.
Get involved and help shape the future of Orchard Core. Ask longer questions, propose ideas, and join the discussion.
Searchable guides, tutorials, and API reference for every module.
Conference talks and tutorials from the people who use and build Orchard Core.
An open online meetup every week. Drop in to demo your work, ask a question, or just listen.

Written and maintained by hundreds of developers around the world.

[contributor_avatars]

ContentItems.DisplayText: Orchard Harvest: Orchard Core's annual conference.

Heading.Text: Orchard Harvest: Orchard Core's annual conference.

Body.Html: Two days in person, with talks and the chance to meet the maintainers and other people building on Orchard Core. This year it is in Vancouver, and tickets are available now.

Lead.Html: Marketing sites, content platforms, intranets, and large multi-tenant applications all run on Orchard Core. Here are just a few of them.

Text.Html: Show Orchard is the community-run gallery of sites and apps built with Orchard Core. Browse it for inspiration, or submit your own.

ContentItems.DisplayText: A few ways to get going.

Title.Text: A few ways to get going.

Lead.Html: Spin up a live site to try it, host one for free, or run it yourself: from the template, an existing ASP.NET Core app, or the Docker image.

CodeSnippet.Text:
[comment]# Install the template, create a site, and run it[/comment]
$ [cmd]dotnet[/cmd] new install [name]OrchardCore.ProjectTemplates[/name]
$ [cmd]dotnet[/cmd] new occms [flag]-n[/flag] [name]MySite[/name]
$ [cmd]cd[/cmd] [name]MySite[/name] && [cmd]dotnet[/cmd] run

[comment]# Run the published image, then open http://localhost:8080[/comment]
$ [cmd]docker[/cmd] run [flag]--name[/flag] [name]orchardcms[/name] \
    [flag]-p[/flag] [name]8080:80[/name] \
    [name]orchardproject/orchardcore-cms-linux:latest[/name]

Body.Html: Spin up a real Orchard Core site to explore the admin. No install, and demo sites are wiped each week.
Want a site that stays? DotNest, an Orchard Core partner, gives you a free hosted site with quick, easy setup.

Text.Html: Read the documentation for guides, tutorials, and API reference, or find out how to get involved and contribute to the project.

Lead.Html: Answers to the things teams ask most before choosing Orchard Core.

Answer.Html: Orchard Core is a free, open-source CMS and application framework built on ASP.NET Core. Content management, users and roles, media, search, workflows, and multi-tenancy are included in the box.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Is Orchard Core free to use?

TitlePart.Title: Is Orchard Core free to use?

Answer.Html: Yes. It is open-source under the BSD-3-Clause license, with no license fees and no per-seat or per-site costs. You also get the full source code.

ContentItems.DisplayText: What can you build with Orchard Core?

TitlePart.Title: What can you build with Orchard Core?

Answer.Html: Content-driven websites, headless back ends served over a GraphQL API, and large multi-tenant applications. The same platform scales from a small site to a large application, with no re-platforming.

ContentItems.DisplayText: What technology is Orchard Core built on?

TitlePart.Title: What technology is Orchard Core built on?

Answer.Html: It is built on ASP.NET Core and extended with C#. Teams build with C# and Razor, host it anywhere .NET runs, and hire from the large pool of .NET developers.

ContentItems.DisplayText: How do I get started with Orchard Core?

TitlePart.Title: How do I get started with Orchard Core?

Answer.Html: Install the project template and create a site with one command, or add the Orchard Core packages to an ASP.NET Core app you already have. The documentation covers both paths.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Who maintains Orchard Core, and is it here for the long term?

TitlePart.Title: Who maintains Orchard Core, and is it here for the long term?

Answer.Html: It is community-led and supported by the .NET Foundation, with a lineage of more than 15 years. No single company owns it, and it has never been relicensed.

Lead.Html: Two days of talks and time with the people who build on Orchard Core, with a chance to meet the maintainers and the wider community. Tickets are available now.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Why come to Orchard Harvest - cards

Body.Html: Ask the people who build Orchard Core in person. Questions that sit in your backlog for months tend to get answered over lunch.
Practical talks from teams running Orchard Core in production, not vendor sessions. Take the ideas straight back to your own projects.
Hear where the project is heading, straight from the people who build it, so you can plan with a clear picture.
Around 30 to 40 people. That means you actually talk to the maintainers and other builders, not wave from row twenty.
Catch anything you miss, and share the talks with the rest of your team when you get back.
Talks span the year's themes: from CMS to content platform, AI and automation, performance and reliability, and what comes next.
Your team brings back ideas to work faster and keep the platform consistent across projects.
Practical talks from experienced users, with better ways to build on Orchard Core and modern .NET.
A clearer read on the roadmap and the ecosystem, so the choices you make hold up over time.
Meet maintainers, experts, and active users, and open the door to future partnerships.

ContentItems.DisplayText: One ticket, the whole team benefits

TitlePart.Title: One ticket, the whole team benefits

Body.Html: Every talk is recorded, so one person can go and bring the notes, ideas, and sessions back to share with everyone.
Both days are $280 (early bird), with practical takeaways the team can use the next week. A focused, modest investment.

Text.Html: The one-pager sums up the case for coming, ready to share with your team.

TitlePart.Title: Why come to Orchard Harvest - cards

Lead.Html: Orchard Harvest is the annual conference for the Orchard Core community: two days of technical talks, product updates, and time with the people who build it. Here is what is in it for you, and for your team.
Our awesome speakers are renowned members of the community and here to bring you the Orchard Core topics your care!

Bio.Html:
Host of Orchard Harvest.

Community member since 2011 and core contributor for more than a decade.

Co-Founder and Managing Director of Lombiq Technologies, mostly focusing on operational, marketing and business development tasks, but still putting his full-stack development and software architecture knowledge to good use as much his other responsibilities allow.

Brian Haydin is a Solution Architect at Concurrency, where he helps organizations design and operationalize practical AI, data, and Microsoft cloud solutions. His work focuses on enterprise AI architecture, agentic application patterns, Microsoft Azure, Microsoft 365, Microsoft Fabric, and the operating models needed to move AI from demo to production. Brian regularly speaks on AgentOps, AI governance, hybrid low-code/pro-code agents, and practical enterprise architecture. He focuses on real-world implementation patterns including observability, evaluations, human-in-the-loop controls, data readiness, and the governance needed to keep AI systems useful, safe, and accountable.

Matt Kenney is the Director of Software, GovBuilt at MCCI, with more than 15 years of experience leading engineering teams and building software businesses in the GovTech and IoT industries. He has helped drive significant company growth, scale development organizations, and contribute to multiple successful acquisitions and business exits. His background includes software architecture, cloud platforms, AI adoption, product development, and turning technology investments into scalable commercial solutions.
As a software engineer, I have a passion for collaborating with the Microsoft Stack, specializing in ASP.NET Core.

For more than 10 years, I have been working on projects built with Microsoft technologies, including ASP.NET, Azure, and SharePoint. I’ve had the opportunity to contribute at leading organizations such as EPAM and 8 West, taking part in challenging projects across diverse industries like banking, oil and gas, automotive, and insurance.

Niraj is a technical lead and architect specializing in scalable, audit‑ready systems. He architects modular, multi‑tenant platforms used across nationwide operations, with a focus on clarity, compliance, and long‑term maintainability. His work leverages Orchard Core as a foundation for building robust enterprise applications that go far beyond traditional CMS use cases. At Harvest, he shares practical patterns and real-world lessons on using Orchard Core to power complex, mission‑critical business workflows.
Fullstack developer in BiQ pux a.s. focusing on delivering custom-made portal solutions.

Founder of Clever Graft (Pty) Ltd, internationally recognized digitization and process automation expert, panelist and contributing author, entrepreneur and coach. He is a combination of a tech evangelist, creator and techpreneur. Experience ranges for large scale systems implementation, venture building and experiments.

Role.Text: Developer; benevolent dictator of Orchard Core, Microsoft

Bio.Html: Developer at Microsoft, benevolent dictator of Orchard Core.

Co-founder and managing director of Lombiq, a company focused on Orchard Core. His main expertise is in Orchard Core development and software architecture design. Active in open-source, core contributor of Orchard Core, guest lecturer at Óbuda University, John von Neumann Faculty of Informatics.

Lead.Html: Two days of talks and community. Times are local to Vancouver.

Description.Html: Leisurely arrive at the venue, grab your badge, and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.
We officially open Orchard Harvest 2026.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Announcement of the Winner of the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award

TitlePart.Title: Announcement of the Winner of the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award

Description.Html: Every year, the community votes on who has done the most for Orchard Core and thus who we can bestow the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award on.
Orchard Core as it is today, a review of what happened since last year, milestones and big events, what's in focus now, and what do we have planned for the foreseeable future.
Coming soon.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Scaling Orchard Core: Lessons from Building and Selling a GovTech Platform

TitlePart.Title: Scaling Orchard Core: Lessons from Building and Selling a GovTech Platform

Description.Html: Learn how GovBuilt leveraged Orchard Core to build a modern government content and application platform that ultimately became part of a successful acquisition. We'll explore the architectural decisions, scaling challenges, and lessons learned while developing more than 170 custom Orchard Core modules across a growing SaaS platform serving local governments throughout North America.
Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket. A few recommendations are coming soon.

Orchard Core gives developers a clean, modular foundation for building real business applications - not just CMS sites. This talk shows practical patterns for structuring custom modules, handling complex business needs, and integrating external services in scalable, maintainable ways.
In this talk I will discuss deploying Orchard-based application as docker containers. I will present also how I will leverage Azure Container Apps to deploy these applications.
Mingle with other community members and grab a cup of coffee and some snacks.

ContentItems.DisplayText: When Workflows Meet Agents: AgentOps Patterns for AI-Enabled .NET Content Platforms

TitlePart.Title: When Workflows Meet Agents: AgentOps Patterns for AI-Enabled .NET Content Platforms

Description.Html: AI agents are starting to show up in content platforms, workflow-driven applications, and automation-heavy business systems, including the kinds of solutions Orchard Core teams build. This session introduces practical AgentOps patterns for safely running those agents in production: tracing behavior, evaluating output quality, managing prompt and tool changes, designing human approval gates, and preventing issues like stale content, context leakage, and untraceable decisions. Attendees will leave with a practical framework for wrapping agents in workflows so AI automation can move beyond demos without turning the platform into a mystery box.
No need to register this time, just arrive at the venue and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Privacy-friendly web analytics in Orchard Core

TitlePart.Title: Privacy-friendly web analytics in Orchard Core

Description.Html: Since GDPR, other privacy laws, and both people and browsers becoming more privacy-aware, Google Analytics is broken. But there's a better way: privacy-friendly, cookie-less analytics that still delivers insights. We'll see how you can do this with the open-source analytics platform Pirsch and the Orchard Core Marketing module.
Coming soon

ContentItems.DisplayText: Green Lights All the Way: A Real-Time SEO Analysis Module for Orchard Core

TitlePart.Title: Green Lights All the Way: A Real-Time SEO Analysis Module for Orchard Core

Description.Html: We built an SEO Analysis Module for Orchard Core that brings actionable insights directly into your CMS dashboard. In this session we'll show how it analyzes published content items for common SEO signals - meta titles, descriptions and more - and surfaces the results through a live dashboard with aggregated health scores across your content library and per-item drill-downs that tell editors exactly what needs fixing and why.
Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket. A few recommendations are coming soon.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Powered by EF core, managed by OC

TitlePart.Title: Powered by EF core, managed by OC

Description.Html: Experience with integrating EF Core into multi-tenanted OC solution. Model management, consumption challenges and an answer.
Submit your questions in advance or bring surprise questions and engage with our expert panel. We'll announce the participants soon.
Mingle with other community members and grab a cup of coffee and some snacks.
We can't reveal any more about it just yet, but you're surely love it!

PresenterRole.Text: Developer; benevolent dictator of Orchard Core, Microsoft

Text.Html: One ticket covers both days, including lunch, breaks and the evening social.

Body.Html: The venue is still being finalised. Orchard Harvest 2026 will be held in Vancouver, Canada - we’ll announce the exact venue, along with travel and accommodation details, soon.

Lead.Html: Last year's Orchard Harvest brought the community to Prague for two days of talks and time between sessions. A look back at the highlights.

MediaTexts: Attendees at last year's Orchard Harvest

Quote.Text: I came away with ideas I could put to work straight away, and with direct relationships with the people who build Orchard Core. Everyone was genuinely friendly - it's rare to get this much time with the community behind a platform you rely on.

Text.Html: Every session is online and free to watch, so you can get a feel for the event.

Lead.Html: The team organizing Orchard Harvest 2026 consists of long-time Orchard community members working on providing you with a great event.
Short answers to what people ask most. Can't find yours? Email contact@orchardcore.net.

Answer.Html: Anyone who builds with Orchard Core, or is thinking about it. That includes developers, technical leads, and the teams around them, from first-timers to long-time contributors.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Do I have to give a talk to come?

TitlePart.Title: Do I have to give a talk to come?

Answer.Html: No. Most people come to listen and meet others. Talks are welcome but never required.
On 10 and 11 September 2026 in Vancouver, Canada. The exact venue will be announced soon; see the venue section for travel and where to stay.
One ticket covers both days: every talk, lunch and breaks, and the evening social. Tickets are $280 (early bird).
It is deliberately small, usually around 30 to 40 people. That is the point: you get real time with the maintainers and other people who build on Orchard Core, not a seat in a crowd.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Can my employer pay, and do you provide a receipt?

TitlePart.Title: Can my employer pay, and do you provide a receipt?

Answer.Html: Yes. Most tickets are paid for by an employer. You get a receipt at checkout, and we can provide an invoice if your finance team needs one. If you need to make the case, the "For your team" tab above has a short email you can send.
Yes. Every talk is recorded and published after the event, so you can catch up on anything you miss and share it with the rest of your team.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Can I get a refund or pass my ticket on?

TitlePart.Title: Can I get a refund or pass my ticket on?

Answer.Html: If you cannot make it, you can pass your ticket to a colleague. Refunds are available up to the date shown at checkout, where the full terms are listed.

ContentItems.DisplayText: How can I ask something else?

TitlePart.Title: How can I ask something else?

Answer.Html: Email contact@orchardcore.net, or ask on the GitHub discussion board where the community can help.

Lead.Html: Questions about the conference, or ideas for it? Here is where to ask, and how to hear about updates first.

Body.Html: Do you have questions about the conference, or ideas for it? Bring them to the discussion board and talk them through with the organizers.
Subscribe to know about updates right away, from speaker announcements to the final schedule.
If you have questions the FAQs do not cover, the organizer team is happy to hear from you directly.

Lead.Html: Orchard Harvest 2025 brought the community to Hotel Botanique in Prague for two days of talks, development plans and ideas, and time with the rest of the worldwide community. This page is an archive of that event.

MediaTexts: Hotel Botanique, the Orchard Harvest 2025 venue in Prague

Lead.Html: The talks came from people who build and use Orchard Core every day. Select a speaker to read their bio.

Bio.Html: Alcwyn Parker is a Senior Web Developer focused on the intersection of technology and user experience. With more than two decades in the software industry and 12 years dedicated to teaching computer science and human-computer interaction, he's passionate about simplifying complex systems. While his expertise spans modern web frameworks like React and VUEJS, C#, Unity, embedded systems, and TypeScript, he has focused extensively on Orchard Core over the last three years, leveraging its capabilities to build intuitive and efficient solutions.

Orchard community member since 2011 and core contributor for more than a decade. Co-Founder and Managing Director of Lombiq Technologies, university guest lecturer since 2014. Full-stack and .NET developer and architect with multiple Orchard-based projects under his belt as a lead developer.

Kryštof is a Frontend Tech Lead at BiQ Pux a.s. from Brno, Czech Republic. He enjoys combining modern JavaScript/TypeScript with ASP.NET Core and strives to teach others that not every feature requires React or other big framework.

Seasoned software engineer, project lead, and consultant with over a decade of experience in the .NET ecosystem, specializing in Orchard Core and ASP.NET Core web applications at Lombiq. Beyond development, I bring expertise in marketing strategy and training for Orchard Core, helping teams adopt and grow with the platform. As a freelancer, I work with organizations to build, refine, and scale their digital products.
Backend developer, Team & Tech lead in BiQ pux a.s.

For more than 10 years, I have been working on projects built with Microsoft technologies, including ASP.NET, Azure, and SharePoint. I’ve had the opportunity to contribute at leading organizations such as EPAM and 8 West, taking part in challenging projects across diverse industries like banking, oil and gas, automotive, and insurance.

Nick Jackson is a Development Manager with 25 years of experience in web development and .NET. For nearly a decade, he has specialised in building robust, scalable solutions using Orchard. Passionate about user-centred design, he focuses on creating seamless, engaging user experiences that deliver real value. Nick is committed to building solutions that are not only technically sound but also intuitive and enjoyable to use.
Fullstack developer in BiQ pux a.s. focusing on delivering custom-made portal solutions.

I'm in love with everything data in .NET - Entity Framework, System.Data, vector databases, and anything else that has you talking to some sort of database. I'm especially interested in making things go faster, and going deep into the guts of things in order to make that happen. I'm also one of the developers working on Npgsql, the PostgreSQL .NET provider.

Sipke is the creator and maintainer of Elsa Workflows, an open-source workflow engine for .NET. As a passionate software developer and contributor to the Orchard Core ecosystem, he enjoys building extensible systems and sharing his knowledge with the community.

Growth Marketing Lead at Easy Software & Easy8.ai, where I focus on scaling organic acquisition using AI, SEO, data and workflow automations. Passionate about connecting marketing and development teams on website development.

Main organizer and host of Orchard Harvest.

As a Business Developer at Lombiq, her work is diverse and varied: She primarily performs Marketing, HR, Organizational and Communication tasks.

Co-founder and managing director of Lombiq. His main expertise is in .NET software development and software architecture design. Active in open-source, core contributor of Orchard Core, guest lecturer at Óbuda University, John von Neumann Faculty of Informatics.

Lead.Html: Times were local to Prague. Sessions were recorded and published on the Orchard YouTube channel.

Description.Html: Leisurely arrive at the venue, grab your badge, and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.
We officially open Orchard Harvest 2025.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Announcement of the Winner of the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award

TitlePart.Title: Announcement of the Winner of the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award

Description.Html: Every year, the community votes on who has done the most for Orchard Core and thus who we can bestow the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award on.
Orchard Core as it is today, a review of what happened since last year, milestones and big events, what's in focus now, and what do we have planned for the foreseeable future.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Orchard Core developer automation with GitHub Actions

TitlePart.Title: Orchard Core developer automation with GitHub Actions

Description.Html: Your code is already on GitHub, so why not automate everything with GitHub Actions too? We’ll see how you can use GitHub Actions to run Orchard Core builds and execute tests, lint everything, run spell-checking, manage an Azure environment, and help contributors.

This talk will demonstrate advanced use cases for sending emails to customers. We’ll explore email queues, failover tasks, and the creation of custom templates with MJML, along with a few extra tips and tweaks.

Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket.

A few recommendations:

  • Botanique Bistro & Bar –offering a daily à la carte menu with a variety of local and international dishes; daily lunch menu every workday (located directly in the hotel)
  • Gate Restaurant – modern restaurant offering Czech and international cuisine, including vegetarian and gluten-free options (about a 3-minute walk)
  • La Bottega Linka– stylish Italian restaurant known for fresh pasta and excellent coffee (approx. 5 minutes away).
  • Café Imperial – elegant art-nouveau restaurant by chef Zdeněk Pohlreich, famous for its traditional Czech dishes and impressive interior (around 10 minutes by walk).

ContentItems.DisplayText: Olystic website case study: A modern CSS + widget workflow for Orchard Core on DotNest

TitlePart.Title: Olystic website case study: A modern CSS + widget workflow for Orchard Core on DotNest

Description.Html: How we turned the Olystic website’s content needs into a reliable set of Orchard Core widgets and UI kit components on DotNest, the hosted Orchard Core SaaS. I’ll walk through the components, the authoring experience, and how we manage assets with a focus on developer experience and code quality.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Supercharging Orchard Core with Elsa Workflows

TitlePart.Title: Supercharging Orchard Core with Elsa Workflows

Description.Html: Discover how the new OrchardCore.ElsaWorkflows module brings the power of Elsa Workflows to Orchard Core. In this session, we will explore how to automate and extend your CMS using workflows that can respond to content events, send and receive HTTP requests, query the database, and more. We’ll also cover how to create custom activities to fully unlock the potential of this seamless integration.
Mingle with other community members and grab a cup of coffee and some snacks.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Using Asp.Net Aspire for Orchard Core dev orchestration

TitlePart.Title: Using Asp.Net Aspire for Orchard Core dev orchestration

Description.Html: In this talk I will present a decoupled CMS using the headless recipe, MySql database and Azure-based shell. I will show how to prepare this environment using ASP.NET Aspire.

ContentItems.DisplayText: How AI and Orchard CMS are bringing programmatic SEO back to life

TitlePart.Title: How AI and Orchard CMS are bringing programmatic SEO back to life

Description.Html: Discover how we leverage programmatic SEO on Orchard CMS to drive organic growth in an era of AI search and zero-click results. I’ll walk through a real-world use case of how we generate hundreds of high-intent landing pages while staying flexible, performant, and AI-discoverable using Orchard. Ideal for marketers and web developers who want to future-proof their SEO strategy.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Harnessing HTML Forms: Simple Widget with Powerful Capabilities

TitlePart.Title: Harnessing HTML Forms: Simple Widget with Powerful Capabilities

Description.Html: Explore innovative ways to utilize HTML forms as dynamic widgets, enhancing user interaction and improving web application functionality. Learn how to transform basic forms into powerful tools that can effectively capture and manage user data.
No need to register this time, just arrive at the venue and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Translating LINQ to SQL: caching, nullability and other deep topics from EF's query pipeline

TitlePart.Title: Translating LINQ to SQL: caching, nullability and other deep topics from EF's query pipeline

Description.Html: Have you ever asked yourself how Entity Framework Core does its magic, translating your LINQ queries all the way to SQL, and reading the results back as .NET objects? In this talk we'll dive deep into the EF pipeline, and discuss the challenges, the architecture and the solutions to some complex problems. We'll discuss performance, SQL optimization, code generation, LINQ expression trees, null semantics headaches, and various fascinating, low-level details that you've thought about when interacting with EF as a user.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Redefining User Experience: Building Flexible, Creator-led Websites with Orchard Core

TitlePart.Title: Redefining User Experience: Building Flexible, Creator-led Websites with Orchard Core

Description.Html: This talk looks at how Buzz Interactive uses Orchard Core to build websites with a strong focus on the editor experience. They share how they approach flexibility in content creation, how that has shaped their use of widgets, and how they have adapted the bag part system to better support their needs.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Orchard Forms for Everyone: Reducing Complexity Through Intuitive Design

TitlePart.Title: Orchard Forms for Everyone: Reducing Complexity Through Intuitive Design

Description.Html: This talk delves into the usability challenges faced by non-technical users when creating forms within Orchard Core and showcases Buzz Interactive's approach to empowering all users, regardless of technical proficiency, to build powerful and engaging forms with ease.

Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket.

A few recommendations:

  • Botanique Bistro & Bar –offering a daily à la carte menu with a variety of local and international dishes; daily lunch menu every workday (located directly in the hotel)
  • Gate Restaurant – modern restaurant offering Czech and international cuisine, including vegetarian and gluten-free options (about a 3-minute walk)
  • La Bottega Linka– stylish Italian restaurant known for fresh pasta and excellent coffee (approx. 5 minutes away).
  • Café Imperial – elegant art-nouveau restaurant by chef Zdeněk Pohlreich, famous for its traditional Czech dishes and impressive interior (around 10 minutes by walk).

ContentItems.DisplayText: Bridging Systems: Seamless Data Integration in OrchardCore

TitlePart.Title: Bridging Systems: Seamless Data Integration in OrchardCore

Description.Html: This presentation presents a custom import module, that provides means to import data from external sources. The content is divided into two key sections. The first section focuses on the process of migrating content from the WordPress platform to the Orchard Core content framework. The second section describes how to import data from external sources (e.g. ERP systems) into SQL tables to be utilized in portal applications.

Submit your questions in advance or bring surprise questions and engage with our expert panel.

Shay Rojansky, Nick Jackson, Chris Durkin and Bertrand Le Roy will be there to answer your questions.
Mingle with other community members and grab a cup of coffee and some snacks.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Maintaining dependencies of Orchard Core apps with Renovate

TitlePart.Title: Maintaining dependencies of Orchard Core apps with Renovate

Description.Html: Maintaining dependencies like NuGet and NPM packages, GitHub Actions references is fu... Fun! But if you feel that it could be even more fun, then you can automate it with a tool called Renovate. We’ll see how you can set up Renovate so it’s useful but not annoying, do selective updates for different dependencies, centralize configuration for all your projects, and use the Renovate App vs GitHub Actions.

ContentItems.DisplayText: From CMS to cross-platform: Orchard Core meets MAUI Blazor Hybrid

TitlePart.Title: From CMS to cross-platform: Orchard Core meets MAUI Blazor Hybrid

Description.Html: In this session, we’ll explore how Orchard Core can power not only websites but also cross-platform apps. Using a single solution, it’s possible to combine an Orchard Core backend with a Blazor Hybrid (MAUI) app and a Blazor WebAssembly app, all sharing common components for a smoother development experience. The result is a flexible setup that lets you build and ship modern applications faster, while reusing the same building blocks across web, desktop, and mobile.

Lead.Html: The team organizing Orchard Harvest 2025 consisted of long-time Orchard community members working on providing a great event.

Lead.Html: Orchard Harvest 2024 brought the community to The Orleans Hotel & Casino in Las Vegas for two days of talks, development plans and ideas, and time with the rest of the worldwide community. This page is an archive of that event.
The talks came from people who build and use Orchard Core every day. Select a speaker to read their bio.

Bio.Html: Orchard community member since 2011 and core contributor for more than a decade. Co-Founder and Managing Director of Lombiq Technologies, university guest lecturer since 2014. Full-stack and .NET developer and architect with multiple Orchard-based projects under his belt as a lead developer.

Burke Holland is a front-end developer living in Nashville, TN; the greatest city in the world according to science. He enjoys JavaScript a lot because it's the only way he Node to Express himself. Get it? Never mind. Burke blogs only slightly better than he codes and definitely not as good as he talks about himself in the third person. Burke works with the VS Code team at Microsoft. You can find him on Twitter as @burkeholland.

Bruno Samardzic is a senior .NET developer and entrepreneur who enjoys working with Orchard Core. He's implemented a number of systems on top of Orchard, including the a main website of one of the leading courier services in the region, IOT hubs and user portals. Been using orchard since v1 onwards.

Drew is a Software Development Manager with 10 years of experience in Software Development. She is excited about Orchard Core and has used it both in her professional work and personal projects like her blog drewbrasher.com. When not creating software or learning cyber security, she enjoys spending time with her wife and children, watching shows/movies, hiking, walking, kayaking, bicycling, and fireside conversations.
Backend developer, Team & Tech lead in BiQ pux a.s.
As a software engineer, I have a passion for collaborating with the Microsoft Stack, specializing in ASP.NET Core and Orchard Core.
Developer at Microsoft, benevolent dictator of Orchard Core.

Since 2000, Sipke has specialized in designing software solutions with a focus on the .NET ecosystem and cloud computing, working on diverse projects nationally and internationally. His career as a freelance developer was spearheaded by extensive work on Orchard projects, leading to his current deep involvement with Elsa Workflows, an open-source .NET project aimed at simplifying workflow creation. Beyond work, Sipke enjoys hiking, mountain biking, reading, gaming, and exploring blockchain technology's potential to transform industries and society.

Toby Dodds is a career web professional based at the Smithsonian Institution in Washington, D.C. As the Director of Web and IT he oversees the technical well-being of the Smithsonian Folkways website and digital collection. He has helped implement numerous innovations over his career and is a lifelong music enthusiast and avid photographer. He holds an undergraduate degree in philosophy from the University of Washington in Seattle, and a Masters in Library and Information Science degree from Catholic University in Washington, D.C.

Main organizer and host of Orchard Harvest.
As a Business Developer at Lombiq, her work is diverse and varied: She primarily performs Marketing, HR, Organizational and Communication tasks.

Co-founder and managing director of Lombiq. His main expertise is in .NET software development and software architecture design. Active in open-source, core contributor of Orchard Core, guest lecturer at Óbuda University, John von Neumann Faculty of Informatics.

Lead.Html: Sessions were recorded and published on the Orchard YouTube channel.

Description.Html: Leisurely arrive at the venue, grab your badge, and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.
We officially open Orchard Harvest 2024.
Orchard Core as it is today, a review of what happened since last year, milestones and big events, what's in focus now, and what do we have planned for the foreseeable future.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Announcement of the Winner of the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award

TitlePart.Title: Announcement of the Winner of the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award

Description.Html: Every year, the community votes on who has done the most for Orchard Core and thus who we can bestow the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award on.

Join us for "Authentication in Orchard Core" to explore secure authentication and authorization within the Orchard Core framework. Learn about Single-Factor and Two-Factor Authentication, integrating external providers, and implementing OpenID Connect for enhanced security and user experience. This session will equip developers and IT professionals with the knowledge to secure their Orchard Core applications effectively.
Shapes are the arcane magic of Orchard Core's theming. Or are they? While they are awesome, there's no magic, and by the end of this session, you'll fully understand them too!
Visit one of the many restaurants and bars of The Orleans and enjoy a meal with the other community members. Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket.

The Content Warning module lets you hide some content behind a warning so that users can decide if they want to view it or not. In this session, you will get to see it in action and take a look at the code that makes it happen. We will see how this is done with a Shortcode as well as a Content Part that adds a wrapper to the parent Content Item.

Submit your questions in advance or bring surprise questions and engage with our expert panel. Mike Alhayek, Toby Dodds, and Burke Holland will be there to answer your questions. Moderated by Sebastien Ros.
Mingle with other community members and grab a cup of coffee and some snacks.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Using Orchard as a Central Portal for the Biggest Small Business Association in SE Europe

TitlePart.Title: Using Orchard as a Central Portal for the Biggest Small Business Association in SE Europe

Description.Html: This talk is a showcase of how we used Orchard to implement this portal and what problems we encountered in the meanwhile. It's now been running for 2 years. What we feel worked, what could be better, and what hacks we resorted to.

ContentItems.DisplayText: From Zero to Azure: Setting Up Your Orchard Application for Success

TitlePart.Title: From Zero to Azure: Setting Up Your Orchard Application for Success

Description.Html: In this talk I'll go through a complete process of setting up an Azure environment tailored for Orchard, enhanced by various open-source components to aid developers and maintainers.
We end the first day of Orchard Harvest 2024.
Join us for our social event for a relaxed evening! The location will be Orleans Hotel LaSalle B.
No need to register this time, just arrive at the venue and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.

Over the course of the last year or so, we've seen numerous improvements to how we function as a community. In this talk, we'll go through what has happened, what effects certain changes had on our community life, and a vision for what we'll see next.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Understanding the Performance Characteristics of Orchard Core

TitlePart.Title: Understanding the Performance Characteristics of Orchard Core

Description.Html: Why is Orchard Core slow when it's slow, why it's fast when it's fast, and how can you figure all of this out? From the performance guy of the ASP.NET Core team.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Smithsonian Folkways Collection Management and Orchard Core Demonstration

TitlePart.Title: Smithsonian Folkways Collection Management and Orchard Core Demonstration

Description.Html: With a mission to increase and diffuse knowledge, Smithsonian Folkways Recordings digitizes and distributes a large catalog of historic sound recordings. At the middle of our enterprise is an Orchard Core implementation that has been customized to support the needs of our collection and website. By blending the role of website CMS with a robust collections management platform, Folkways has achieved its goals of broadly distributing its collection via its website and through multiple 3rd party music services and platforms - reaching millions of listeners per year.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Unlocking Hyperproductivity with Orchard, Elsa, and Intelligent Agents

TitlePart.Title: Unlocking Hyperproductivity with Orchard, Elsa, and Intelligent Agents

Description.Html: In this session, we will explore the integration of Orchard Core, Elsa Workflows, and intelligent agents to revolutionize content management. You will learn how to harness the power of AI to automate and enhance various stages of content creation and publishing using workflows and agents, achieving unprecedented productivity.
Visit one of the many restaurants and bars of The Orleans and enjoy a meal with the other community members. Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Security Scanning an Orchard Core Application

TitlePart.Title: Security Scanning an Orchard Core Application

Description.Html: As a web developer, you have tons of security aspects to keep in mind: preventing XSS and SQL injection, configuring HTTPS and HSTS, setting up the proper CSP headers… While you do need to understand these, you have help doing them right: security scanning! In this talk, I'll show you how you can scan your Orchard Core app for security issues with Zed Attack Proxy, also doing it automatically as part of UI testing.

The talk's focus is on speeding up custom development via modules that bring new possibilities to Orchard Core. We have developed a module for creation & migration of DB tables, a module to fill these tables via CSV/Excel import and a module that shows them in dynamic tables with filters, sorters and more. All editable in admin, (almost) no-code required, so developers can focus on more complex tasks. Still a WIP though.
Collaborate and innovate with fellow attendees over coffee and snacks. We'll bring some Orchard Core issues that you'll be able to work on with fellow community members, for all skill levels.
We end the second day of Orchard Harvest 2024.

Lead.Html: Orchard Harvest 2023 was the first online Orchard Harvest, starting on the 31st of May at 1 PM UTC. It was a great opportunity to share knowledge, talk about development plans and ideas, and meet the rest of the worldwide community. This page is an archive of that event.
The talks came from people who build and use Orchard Core every day. Select a speaker to read their bio.

Bio.Html: F# tech lead in Lula Technologies Inc., co-founder of The Secret Circle Solutions, lecturer in IT Step computer academy, Azure architect, maintainer of Elmish.Uno F# framework for Uno Platform, co-author of Surface Dial Tools and GuidFac extensions for Visual Studio.
A software engineer at Lombiq and the project leader of the Orchard Core Commerce development team.

Core contributor of Orchard Core, maintainer of Orchard Core Contrib and lecturer at Al-Rayan & Al-Arab Universities. His main expertise is localization. Hisham did massive localization contributions in many OSS such ASP.NET Core, ASP.NET Docs, Orchard Core, Oqtane Framework and SimplCommerce, etc.

Founder and president of Instant Technologies. Instant technologies has been providing IM, and chat based, enterprise applications since 2001. Instant has developed more than 10 enterprise applications across a variety of platforms (Sametime, Notes, Skype, Slack, MS Teams, O365). Prior to starting Instant, Peyton was a product manager, and developer, at Lotus\IBM including an onsite assignment at SAP.
Developer at Microsoft, benevolent dictator of Orchard Core.

Sipke Schoorstra is a software developer with a focus on .NET software development and architecture. Between 2010 and 2016, Sipke made valuable contributions to the Orchard project. Additionally, he is known as the creator of Elsa Workflows, an open-source .NET library for creating workflows-enabled applications.

Main organizer and host of Orchard Harvest.
As a Business Developer at Lombiq, her work is diverse and varied: She primarily performs Marketing, HR, Organizational and Communication tasks.

Co-founder and managing director of Lombiq. His main expertise is in .NET software development and software architecture design. Active in open-source, core contributor of Orchard Core, guest lecturer at Obuda University, John von Neumann Faculty of Informatics.

Lead.Html: Sessions were recorded and published on the Orchard YouTube channel.

Description.Html: We get everyone settled and open the conference.
Mostly jokes, not sure which ones yet.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Using Orchard Core Framework for multi-tenancy apps

TitlePart.Title: Using Orchard Core Framework for multi-tenancy apps

Description.Html: What is muti-tenancy? How doest it work in Orchard Core? Setting up another tenant. Per-tenant settings and services. Configuring the tenant URL. How to call one tenant from another one?

ContentItems.DisplayText: Automated QA for Orchard Core apps

TitlePart.Title: Automated QA for Orchard Core apps

Description.Html: Are you sure your Orchard Core app is still working after the last commit? Manual testing is always necessary, but automated Quality Assurance is a must. In this talk, we'll go through the lines of defense that you can utilize to ensure that your app doesn't break. You'll see how you can use open-source tools in the following areas: CI builds, static code analysis, unit and UI testing, telemetry collection.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Leveraging Orchard to rebuild, and reimagine, our enterprise service desk application

TitlePart.Title: Leveraging Orchard to rebuild, and reimagine, our enterprise service desk application

Description.Html: 18 months ago, I decided that our chat based service desk application required an overhaul. Our new application would be multi-tenant, SAAS, include a CMS, and enable us to quickly onboard new customers. This session will discuss why we selected Orchard, how our team has leverage Orchard to rebuild, and re-imagine, our application, and how we will continue to leverage Orchard for our next set of features. Our current customers are large, multi-national corporations with employees and service desk agents across multiple countries, languages, and continents.

Your chance to meet the speakers and other community members. Each speaker will have a breakout room where you can ask questions and discuss the topic of their talk, or anything else Orchard Core related.

ContentItems.DisplayText: Orchard Core Localization from Zero to Hero

TitlePart.Title: Orchard Core Localization from Zero to Hero

Description.Html: This talk will explore different parts of localization stuff in Orchard Core. We'll go through the process of how to make the app localization ready, then extract and manage the localization resources then using various localization services and UI components. After that, we'll look how to localize the content of your website. Finally, we might cover some of the future features including data localization.

Orchard Core Commerce is the e-commerce solution for Orchard Core. It's the spiritual successor to Nwazet Commerce for Orchard 1. This talk will describe the project's state and demo the current features.

Join me for an exploration of Elsa Workflows, an innovative workflow engine derived from Orchard Core's Workflows module. This session will provide an overview of Elsa's features, compare it to OC Workflows, and discuss integration possibilities. Discover Elsa's visual designer, code-based workflows, versioning capabilities, composite activities, and sub workflows. Uncover the similarities and differences between OC and Elsa Workflows, and learn about Elsa's origins as an independent class library project. Gain insights into seamlessly integrating Elsa back into Orchard Core, combining the best of both worlds. Join me as we unlock the potential of Elsa Workflows and enhance Orchard Core with its remarkable capabilities.
We end Orchard Harvest 2023.

Lead.Html: The team organizing Orchard Harvest 2023 consisted of long-time Orchard community members working on providing a great event.

Heading.Text: Built by the community, with help from our partners.

Intro.Html: These partners support the project in different ways, alongside the community that builds and maintains it.

Tagline.Html: The open, modular .NET CMS and application framework. BSD-3-Clause licensed, a .NET Foundation project.
